title: 3 High Yield Utility Stocks With Safe Dividends
author: Bob Ciura
description: The article recommends three utility stocks as defensive investments against potential economic downturns driven by persistent inflation and geopolitical tensions. American Electric Power (AEP) exceeded earnings expectations with strong revenue and raised its dividend while guiding higher EPS growth. Consolidated Edison (ED) reported revenue growth and has increased dividends for 52 consecutive years, qualifying as a Dividend King. American Water Works (AWK) raised its dividend by 8.2% and reaffirmed its long-term growth targets, with consistent revenue increases driven by rate case wins.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Exceeded Q4 2025 earnings expectations with strong revenue performance, raised quarterly dividend to $0.95 per share, guided 2026 EPS to $6.15-$6.45 range, and announced ambitious $72 billion five-year capital plan with doubled contracted pipeline to 56 gigawatts by 2030.

Reported 6.3% revenue growth to $5.1 billion exceeding estimates, expects high single-digit annual rate base growth through 2030, planning $38 billion capital investments over five years, and has achieved Dividend King status with 52 consecutive years of dividend increases. Currently yields 3.3%.

Raised dividend by 8.2% to $0.895 quarterly rate, reported 5.7% revenue growth to $1.21 billion driven by rate case wins and acquisitions, reaffirmed FY2026 adjusted EPS guidance of $6.02-$6.12, and maintains 7-9% long-term EPS and dividend growth targets. Currently yields 2.9%.

title: Top 3 Energy Dividend Stocks for Reliable Income in 2026
author: Jack Delaney
description: The article highlights three energy sector stocks with strong dividend track records: Consolidated Edison (a Dividend King with 52 years of consecutive increases), Enbridge (31 years of increases with a 4.8% yield), and Enterprise Products Partners (27 years of increases with a 5.5% yield). Despite energy sector volatility, these companies maintain reliable income generation through regulated utilities, diversified energy approaches, and midstream services.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Recognized as a Dividend King with 52+ years of consecutive dividend increases. Strong net income growth ($1.8B in 2024 to $2B in 2025) and solid 3.2% dividend yield supported by regulated utility business model providing consistent cash flow.

31 years of consecutive dividend increases with attractive 4.8% yield. Diversified energy portfolio including renewable and natural gas operations. Strong GAAP earnings of CA$7B ($5B USD) in 2025 support sustainable dividend growth.

27 years of consecutive dividend increases with high 5.5% yield. Positioned well in growing natural gas market (expected to grow from $895B in 2025 to $1T+ by 2033). Consistent net income ($5.8-5.9B annually) demonstrates dividend sustainability despite high yield.
Mentioned only as a customer of Enbridge's renewable energy project in Texas; no investment recommendation or analysis provided.

title: CON EDISON DECLARES COMMON STOCK DIVIDEND
author: Prnewswire
description: Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE:ED) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.8875 per share on its common stock, payable on June 15, 2026 to shareholders of record as of May 13, 2026. The company is a holding company providing electric, gas, and steam services through its subsidiaries in New York and New Jersey.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The declaration of a quarterly dividend demonstrates the company's financial stability and commitment to returning value to shareholders. Regular dividend payments are typically viewed positively by income-focused investors and indicate confidence in the company's cash flow generation.

title: Electric, Natural Gas and Combination Utilities See Customer Experience Gains Stall as Billing Friction Reemerges
author: Escalent
description: Customer Effort scores for US utilities declined by one point year-over-year to 722, signaling a setback driven primarily by billing and payment friction. Despite the overall decline, 31 utilities were recognized as 'Easiest to Do Business With,' with leading practices including improved bill transparency and customer support programs. The decline reflects affordability pressures and the need for utilities to better communicate billing information and provide payment flexibility.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Recognized as 'Easiest to Do Business With' with a Customer Effort score of 739 (top decile). Highlighted as a leading example for offering multilingual bill explainers, video explanations, and integrated savings programs.
Recognized as 'Easiest to Do Business With' with a Combination service Customer Effort score of 734, demonstrating above-average customer experience.
Combination utility with a Customer Effort score of 724, which is below the top performers but above the industry average of 722.
Recognized as 'Easiest to Do Business With' with a Combination service Customer Effort score of 744.
Multiple Duke Energy regional entities show mixed performance, with scores ranging from 713-735, generally near or slightly above industry average but not in top decile.

title: My Top 3 Dividend Kings to Buy for March 2026
author: Patrick Sanders
description: The article highlights three Dividend King stocks recommended for March 2026: AbbVie, a pharmaceutical company with strong drug pipeline and 54 years of consecutive dividend increases; Walmart, a retail powerhouse with growing e-commerce and 53 years of dividend growth; and Consolidated Edison, a utility company with predictable cash flow and 52 years of consecutive dividend increases.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong pipeline with 90 drug candidates, successful revenue growth from Skyrizi and Rinvoq offsetting Humira patent loss, 54-year dividend streak, and 3% dividend yield make it an attractive dividend investment.

Global retail powerhouse with $713 billion in sales, strong e-commerce growth (27% U.S., 17% international), 53-year dividend streak, and impressive 46% stock price gain over 12 months support the recommendation.

Utility company with predictable cash flow, significant capital investments ($6.6-6.8 billion annually), growing demand from electrification and data centers, 52-year dividend streak, and 3.1% yield make it a stable dividend choice.
Mentioned for context in retail comparison; article notes Amazon surpassed Walmart in total sales but argues Walmart's retail-only sales are larger when excluding AWS cloud computing segment.
